title: Inverse-Designed Tapers for Compact Conversion Between Single-Mode and Wide Waveguides
summary: Waveguide tapers are critical components for leveraging the benefits of both single-mode and wide waveguides. Adiabatic tapers are typically hundreds of microns in length, dramatically limiting density and scalability. We reenvision the taper design process in an inverse-design paradigm, introducing the novel L-taper. We present a novel approach to inverse-designed tapers where the input and output waveguides are rotated 90 degrees with respect to each other. The resultant design has an order-of-magnitude smaller footprint, and the design process is compatible with a variety of fabrication processes. We demonstrate an L-taper designed on 220 nm silicon-on-insulator that converts a 0.5 micron waveguide to a 12 micron waveguide with -0.38 dB transmission and 40 nm 1-dB bandwidth. The footprint is 16 micron by 6 micron, representing a 12x smaller footprint than a linear taper with the same transmission.
